title: Which Nicolas Cage Character Are You, Actually?
dek: Eight Cage performances, zero chill — answer honestly and find out which unhinged icon lives in your soul.

quiz.intro: No wrong answers here — just go with whatever your gut screams first.

questions.question: It's Friday night. What's the vibe?

answers.text: Steal something dumb for love. It'll work out.
Fly a plane full of convicts home. No big deal.
Snort, drink, and howl at the moon in a snakeskin jacket.
Drink until the sun comes up. That's the whole plan.

questions.question: How would your friends describe your energy?

answers.text: Big, loud, and secretly a huge softie for his kid.
Chaotic, horny, and quoting Elvis unprompted.
Charming right up until it's devastating.
Unpredictable in a way that scares people, honestly.
This whole world is wild at heart and weird on top.
Some things you just don't come back from — and that's okay.
Chaos is just order nobody's figured out yet.
History is a treasure hunt if you look hard enough.

questions.question: Pick a soundtrack needle-drop for your life:

answers.text: A slow, aching jazz standard nobody wants to admit makes them cry.
Opera, blasted way too loud, during something violent.
Triumphant orchestral swell over an old dusty document.
Awkward silence. No music. Just my own inner monologue.

questions.question: Your biggest flaw, if we're being honest?

answers.text: I take 'method acting' way too literally.
I will absolutely commit a felony for the right cause.
I sabotage myself before anyone else gets the chance.
I've been a little too obsessed with my own legacy.

questions.question: How do you handle a crisis?

answers.text: Make a completely insane, over-researched plan on the spot.
Spiral internally for way too long before doing anything.
Ask myself: what would my most iconic role do here?
Panic, then improvise something surprisingly heartfelt.
Writing about you but never actually telling you.
Reconnecting with my kid by literally saving her life.
Kidnapping a baby because I want us to be a family so bad.
Literally storming a prison plane for you.

questions.question: Someone challenges you at a party. You:

answers.text: Turn it into a bit, then genuinely get a little hurt.
Laugh it off, then feel weirdly guilty about it later.
Stay quiet and polite until it's suddenly a fistfight.
Get way too into it, in the best/worst way.

questions.question: What's your idea of a happy ending?

answers.text: A regular life, a family, no more running from the law.
Landing the plane and hugging my daughter.
Getting the girl and driving off into the sunset, wild as ever.
Being truly seen by one other person, even briefly.

results.name: H.I. McDunnough
results.subtitle: Raising Arizona (1987)
results.description: You're a repeat offender with a heart of gold, chasing an ordinary family life you're convinced you don't deserve. You mean well, you love hard, and you will absolutely commit one (1) crime if it means keeping your family together.

results.name: Cameron Poe
results.subtitle: Con Air (1997)
results.description: You're quiet, disciplined, and terrifyingly capable when it counts. All you actually want is to get home to your family — everything else, including a plane full of the worst criminals in America, is just an obstacle in the way.

results.name: Sailor Ripley
results.subtitle: Wild at Heart (1990)
results.description: You're pure reckless romance in a snakeskin jacket, allergic to boring and incapable of sitting still. Life with you is loud, a little dangerous, and never, ever dull.

results.name: Ben Sanderson
results.subtitle: Leaving Las Vegas (1995)
results.description: You feel everything a little too much and you've made peace with the parts of yourself you can't fix. There's a heaviness to you, but also a strange, aching tenderness that makes people love you anyway.

results.name: Castor Troy
results.subtitle: Face/Off (1997)
results.description: You are chaos with a grin on it — dramatic, dangerous, and having way too much fun doing it. Nobody's ever bored around you, though they might be a little afraid.

results.name: Benjamin Franklin Gates
results.subtitle: National Treasure (2004)
results.description: You're the earnest history nerd who somehow turns a museum tour into a felony. Your excitement is contagious, your plans are borderline illegal, and you always, always crack the code.

results.name: Charlie (& Donald) Kaufman
results.subtitle: Adaptation (2002)
results.description: You're anxious, self-critical, and probably overthinking this very quiz right now. There's a whole other, more confident version of you living somewhere in your head — and honestly, you kind of resent how easy things are for him.

results.name: Nick Cage (himself, sort of)
results.subtitle: The Unbearable Weight of Massive Talent (2022)
results.description: You're self-aware enough to laugh at your own legend, but you still secretly want everyone to remember how good you actually are. Deep down you just want your family back — and maybe one more great part to prove it.

sources.title: The Unbearable Weight of Massive Talent - Wikipedia
